title: sports-registry — compliance evidence
description: The sports registry carries reference data (taxonomy names + slugs + governing bodies). No PII on any row. Zero third-party subprocessors. The compliance surface is narrow: mostly audit-trail integrity + data-minimisation attestation.

controls.evidence: The catalog stores only taxonomy metadata (name, slug, category, governing body). No user data, no athlete data, no PII of any kind. The `data/platform-sports-catalog.json` file is a static list of public sport identifiers — safe to ship as a bundled artifact.

Platform-seeded rows are permanent — but they carry no PII, so storage limitation doesn't apply to them under GDPR. Tenant customs follow the 730-day deactivation retention window (see retention.json).

Every Registry mutation is `Auditable`; every change writes to `audits` with actor, timestamp, changed_fields, request_id, before + after snapshots. Regular mutations retained 2 years; SportPrefixResolutionFailed events retained 7 years for compliance replay.

Every write path is behind SportPolicy / DisciplinePolicy / PositionPolicy. Platform rows are strictly read-only on the tenant plane. Custom writes are gated by both a permission (admin/owner) + an entitlement (sports_registry_custom, Enterprise-only). Cross-tenant references are refused at the observer + at the DB partial unique index layer.

controls.principle: authorisation of changes to system components
controls.evidence: Slugs are IMMUTABLE post-create — a slug rename would silently orphan every downstream reference. is_platform_seeded is IMMUTABLE — any attempted flip is an audit-critical anomaly. tenant_id + parent references (sport_id / discipline_id) are IMMUTABLE. Every attempted violation writes to audits + fires a warn-severity SportPrefixResolutionFailed or dedicated observer refusal.

10 health probes cover schema migration, catalog JSON parse-ability, platform catalog seeded state, tenant FK integrity, slug uniqueness, parent FK integrity, cross-tenant reference integrity (SECURITY-critical), resolver reachability, reconciler recency, drift-count bounded, purge queue drain. The DriftCountBounded probe catches downstream Wave 3 schema drift that would otherwise silently accumulate.
